role_officer_desc: Full access — analytics, trends & disease monitoring

version: Rubber Tree Disease Prediction System v2.2
hint_1: Upload leaf photo from left panel
Online mode recommended for full details: Online mode recommended for full details.
Connect to server for detailed treatment: Connect to server for detailed treatment info.
Avoid wounding during tapping — use shar: Avoid wounding during tapping — use sharp clean knives
Waterlogged soils, excessive tapping, po: Waterlogged soils, excessive tapping, poorly drained fields.
Combination of Phytophthora spp. infecti: Combination of Phytophthora spp. infection, drought stress, and nutrient deficiency.
Remove all fallen infected leaves from f: Remove all fallen infected leaves from field
Keep field records of any changes in lea: Keep field records of any changes in leaf color or texture
Repeated defoliation leading to branch d: Repeated defoliation leading to branch die-back
Irrigate field if drought-related; impro: Irrigate field if drought-related; improve drainage if waterlogged
Ensure proper field drainage before plan: Ensure proper field drainage before planting
Apply scheduled fertilization as per cro: Apply scheduled fertilization as per crop calendar
Apply preventive fungicide sprays in hig: Apply preventive fungicide sprays in high-risk seasons
Dark brown to black necrotic lesions on: Dark brown to black necrotic lesions on leaf margins
Remove and burn infected leaves immediat: Remove and burn infected leaves immediately
Trunk injection of fungicides for severe: Trunk injection of fungicides for severe cases
Reduce canopy density to improve air cir: Reduce canopy density to improve air circulation
Large brown irregular spots with yellow: Large brown irregular spots with yellow borders
Can cause 20-40% yield reduction if untr: Can cause 20-40% yield reduction if untreated during outbreak season.
One of the most devastating rubber disea: One of the most devastating rubber diseases — can cause up to 60% yield loss.
Immediate action required — spread is ra: Immediate action required — spread is rapid
Grayish fungal sporulation on lesion sur: Grayish fungal sporulation on lesion surface
Maintain field sanitation — remove falle: Maintain field sanitation — remove fallen leaves
Dark water-soaked lesions on bark near s: Dark water-soaked lesions on bark near soil level
Avoid monoculture planting in disease-en: Avoid monoculture planting in disease-endemic zones
Monitor for 1 week; apply treatment if n: Monitor for 1 week; apply treatment if not improving
Yellowing and drying of leaf edges (marg: Yellowing and drying of leaf edges (margin scorch)
Avoid overhead irrigation; use drip irri: Avoid overhead irrigation; use drip irrigation
Apply bark protectants annually during r: Apply bark protectants annually during rainy season
Ensure proper canopy management and prun: Ensure proper canopy management and pruning
Spray Hexaconazole (0.1%) or Propiconazo: Spray Hexaconazole (0.1%) or Propiconazole every 10 days
No impact — tree is in healthy condition: No impact — tree is in healthy condition.
Fungal pathogen Corynespora cassiicola,: Fungal pathogen Corynespora cassiicola, extremely aggressive in susceptible clones.
Temperatures 25-32°C, high relative humi: Temperatures 25-32°C, high relative humidity, wet weather.
Apply balanced NPK fertilizer (12:6:20): Apply balanced NPK fertilizer (12:6:20) to recover nutrition
No visible lesions, spots, or discolorat: No visible lesions, spots, or discoloration
Sunken lesions with yellow halo rings: Sunken lesions with yellow halo rings
Pink-orange spore masses (acervuli) in h: Pink-orange spore masses (acervuli) in humid conditions
Spray Mancozeb (0.2%) or Carbendazim (0.1%) on affected areas
Apply copper-based fungicide (Bordeaux m: Apply copper-based fungicide (Bordeaux mixture 1%) at 2-week intervals
Apply Metalaxyl or Fosetyl-Al (Aliette): Apply Metalaxyl or Fosetyl-Al (Aliette) to soil and bark
Reduce tapping frequency on stressed tre: Reduce tapping frequency on stressed trees
Paint trunk with Bordeaux paste (10%) on: Paint trunk with Bordeaux paste (10%) on lesion areas
Persistent infection can kill tapping pa: Persistent infection can kill tapping panels, reducing latex yields by 30-50%.
Brown papery texture of affected leaves: Brown papery texture of affected leaves
Fungal pathogen Colletotrichum gloeospor: Fungal pathogen Colletotrichum gloeosporioides, spread by wind-borne spores and rain splash.
Die-back of tender shoots in severe case: Die-back of tender shoots in severe cases
Apply prophylactic fungicide sprays befo: Apply prophylactic fungicide sprays before rainy season
Consult local agricultural officer for s: Consult local agricultural officer for specific treatment details.

err_invalid_image_desc: Please provide a clear, close-up photo of a Rubber Tree leaf for accurate diagnosis.
